pi_items.source: task.json description explicitly instructs the agent to use 'Royal Observatory Greenwich'.
task.json description explicitly instructs the agent to use Latitude: 51.4769° N.
task.json description explicitly instructs the agent to use Longitude: 0.0005° W (which is -0.0005°).
verifier.py defines GREENWICH_LAT_RAD = 0.8984, which matches math.radians(51.4769).
verifier.py defines GREENWICH_LON_RAD = 0.0000, which matches math.radians(-0.0005) rounded to 4 decimal places.
task.json description explicitly instructs the agent to use Altitude: 47m.
Web search confirms the 2022 Mars retrograde cycle started Oct 30, 2022, reached opposition Dec 8, 2022, and ended retrograde Jan 12, 2023. The task.json description also lists these exact dates.
task.json description explicitly instructs the agent to use 22:00 UTC for all observations.

task.json description explicitly instructs the agent to disable atmosphere, disable ground, enable ecliptic, and enable constellation lines. verifier.py checks exactly these 4 flags from the Stellarium config.
task.json description explicitly states to write the notes to /home/ga/Desktop/mars_retrograde_notes.txt. export_result.sh reads from this file.
task.json description states Stellarium saves screenshots to /home/ga/Pictures/stellarium/. export_result.sh checks this directory for new screenshots.
task.json description asks for screenshots at 3 key dates. verifier.py requires new_ss >= 3 for full points.

privileged_info_summary: The task requires configuring Stellarium to simulate the 2022-2023 Mars retrograde motion from the Royal Observatory Greenwich. It involves setting the location to latitude 51.4769° N (0.8984 rad), longitude 0.0005° W (0.0000 rad), and altitude 47m. The agent must disable the atmosphere and landscape flags, and enable the ecliptic and constellation drawing flags. Three screenshots are required at 22:00 UTC on the astronomically accurate dates of October 30, 2022 (retrograde begins), December 8, 2022 (opposition), and January 12, 2023 (retrograde ends). The screenshots must be saved to /home/ga/Pictures/stellarium and teaching notes written to /home/ga/Desktop/mars_retrograde_notes.txt.
